title: Activity Detectables
description: A list of detectable applications used for activity detection. Each entry maps one or more process executables to a display name and an icon URL, used solely to identify a running application.

name.description: Display name shown for this application, e.g. "Minecraft".

aliases.description: Optional. Alternate names and search terms for this application, e.g. ["Minecraft Launcher", "Minecraft: Java Edition"].

url.description: HTTPS URL to the application's icon. Should point at a first-party / official source; clients fetch it lazily at runtime.

executables.description: Process executables that identify this application. At least one is required for the entry to be detectable. Match if ANY executable matches.

name.description: Process to match. Stored lowercase for case-insensitive matching. Three forms: (1) a bare executable name, e.g. "spotify.exe"; (2) a path suffix using forward slashes, matched against the tail of the process path, e.g. "content/minecraft.exe"; (3) a ">"-prefixed name, e.g. ">javaw.exe", used together with `arguments` to pin a shared runtime to a specific application.

os.description: Operating system this executable applies to. Values match Node's process.platform.

arguments.description: Optional. A command-line substring the process must be running with to match, e.g. "net.minecraft.client.main.Main". Pair with a ">"-prefixed `name` to disambiguate shared runtimes such as java/javaw, so they are only detected when launching this specific application.
